intended_use: Trained medical professionals use Contour ProtégéAI as a tool to assist in the automated processing of digital medical images of modalities CT and MR, as supported by ACR/NEMA DICOM 3.0. Creation of c
intended_use_full: Trained medical professionals use Contour ProtégéAI as a tool to assist in the automated processing of digital medical images of modalities CT and MR, as supported by ACR/NEMA DICOM 3.0. Creation of contours using machine-learning algorithms for applications including, but not limited to, quantitative analysis, aiding adaptive therapy, transferring contours to radiation therapy treatment planning systems, and archiving contours for patient follow-up and management. Segmenting anatomical structures across a variety of CT anatomic locations. And segmenting the prostate, the seminal vesicles, and the urethra within T2-weighted MR images.
ground_truth: Manual segmentations by physicians and dosimetrists, reviewed and corrected by radiation oncologists against published clinical contouring guidelines
other_metrics: Dice coefficients reported for anatomical structures ranging from 0.03 to 0.98; mean Dice scores for Contour ProtégéAI equivalent or superior to MIM Atlas reference device across all tested structures
